message:
feat(fcp-core): extract tool_schema module from fcp-cli into shared crate

Move the tool-schema codec (MCP, Claude, OpenAI format generation) out
of the CLI-only fcp-cli crate into fcp-core so it can be reused by any
crate in the workspace.

Key changes:
- New crates/fcp-core/src/tool_schema.rs (420 lines): canonical
  OperationInfo→tool-schema conversion for all three AI runtime formats,
  with its own ExportOptions, struct definitions, and description builder
- crates/fcp-core/src/lib.rs: expose tool_schema as a public module
- crates/fcp-cli/src/main.rs: remove the now-redundant local tool_schema
  module declaration
- crates/fwc/src/export_tools.rs: refactor to_mcp_tool, to_claude_tool,
  and to_openai_tool to delegate to fcp_core::tool_schema instead of
  duplicating the conversion logic inline; add helper functions
  (to_operation_info, parse_risk_level, parse_safety_tier,
  parse_idempotency, parse_approval_mode, shared_export_options) to
  bridge the DiscoveredOperation catalog format to the canonical
  OperationInfo contract; gate the now-unused local helpers
  (make_tool_name, build_description, is_read_only, is_destructive)
  behind #[cfg(test)] so existing tests still compile
- crates/fwc/src/agent_coord.rs: clarify doc comments on inbox(),
  read_inbox(), and unread_count() to accurately describe non-consuming
  peek vs draining read semantics

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
